name: Washington State Grange v. Washington State Republican Party
summary: The Supreme Court upheld Washington State's nonpartisan blanket primary against a facial constitutional challenge, applying the principle that a law with a 'plainly legitimate sweep' is not facially invalid even if some hypothetical applications might raise legal concerns.
significance: Justice Sotomayor referenced the 'plainly legitimate sweep' standard — that possible impermissible applications cannot render a rule facially invalid so long as the rule has a substantial range of permissible applications — as an intermediate standard the ATF rule would also satisfy.
